title: Fix a suite where child processes outlive the test owner
prompt: A test suite has this pattern: `setup do {:ok, pid} = MyApp.Worker.start_link([]); {:ok, worker: pid} end` — the Worker is unlinked from the test process. On suite termination, remaining workers sometimes query the Repo and produce `DBConnection.OwnershipError`. Rewrite the setup at `test/my_app/worker_outlives_test.exs`: use `start_supervised!({MyApp.Worker, []})` instead of `start_link/1`. Add a brief comment explaining the fix.
tier_rationale: Lifetime management fix with a subtle discriminator (start_link vs start_supervised).
research_citation: "These ownership errors usually happen when a GenServer outlives the process that called `checkout`" — Elixir Forum, al2o3cr
